title: ficus neriifolia var. nemoralis
intro: Ficus neriifolia var. nemoralis, also known as the Indian laurel or banyan fig, is a variety of flowering plant in the fig family that is native to tropical and subtropical regions. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as fever, diarrhea, and skin infections. The health benefits of Ficus neriifolia var. nemoralis include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce stress, and boost the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and bark in hot water or alcohol to create a medicinal tea or liquid extract.
